content.main_text_preview: Important Alerts | Screen Reader | Skip to Main Content | A- A A+ Institute Portal MIS Login Web Mail Contact Us Student Portal (old) SHRI GOVINDRAM SEKSARIA INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY & SCIENCE, INDORE An Autonomous Institute Run by Govt. of Madhya Pradesh Affiliated to Rajiv Gandhi Proudyogiki Vishwavidyalaya, Bhopal & Devi Ahilya Vishwavidyalaya, Indore Home STUDENT Departmental Club Latest News & Events Site Visit Report: Dynamic Pile Load Testing Using PDA at Omaxe City, Indore 12-01-2026 Expert Lecture on Modern Construction Practices and Sustainable Building Materials 13-11-2025 Civil Engineering Celebrates as SGSITS Women Triumph in Inter-College Kabaddi Championship 30-10-2025 SGSITS Civil Engineering Department Hosts Technical Quiz Competition on Engineers' Day 20-10-2025 View More Departmental Club Preamble With an objective to foster holistic development, leadership qualities, teamwork, and professional excellence among students of Civil Engineering & Applied Mechanics at SGSITS Indore, the department hereby constitutes various student-driven clubs. These clubs will serve as platforms for extracurricular, co-curricular, and technical engagements, thereby nurturing creativity, innovation, communication, and professional competencies in line with the institute’s vision. Objectives - To provide opportunities for students to develop their literary, cultural, technical, and sporting skills. - To bridge classroom learning with industry practices through technical activities, expert talks, internships, and placements. - To promote awareness and use of standards, codes, and best practices in civil engineering. - To encourage teamwork, leadership, ethics, and social responsibility among students. - To enhance the overall student experience through constructive engagement beyond academics. 1. Structure of Clubs The following clubs are established under the Department of Civil Engineering & Applied Mechanics: 1.1 Literary Club (M Vishveshvaraya Club) Faculty Coordinator :- Prof S K Ahirwar, Dr Arti Sahu, Dr Akash Jaiswal Aim: To develop communication, expression, and documentation skills. Activities: Newsletter, magazine design, articles, creative writing, debates. For Example Departmental newsletter and magazine design. Articles, blogs, and technical write-ups. Debates, essay competitions, creative writing Department Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn pages etc. 1.2 Cultural Club (Trivim Club) Faculty Coordinator :- Asst. Prof. Tarun Narnaure, Asst. Prof. Suninda Parmar, Asst. Prof. Megha Chourey Aim: To preserve cultural values, encourage creativity, and provide a platform for self-expression. Activities: Singing, dance, drama, cultural evenings, street plays, Reels and online streaming For Example Singing, dance, drama, instrumental performances. Festival celebrations, cultural evenings, and talent showcases. Street plays and awareness programs. Reels and Managing Department YouTube Channel, FB/Instagram/Twitter 1.3 STIMULUS Club Faculty Coordinator: - Prof S M Narulkar , Er Vivek Tiwari PG Students ( 2nd Year STR/TRE/ENV) (a) Technical Club (Stimulus Training & Skill Development Cell ) Aim: To enhance technical knowledge, employability, and professional development. Activities: Skill development, expert lectures, GD, mock interviews, aptitude sessions. For Example Skill development programs (software, tools, and modern construction techniques). Guest lectures, expert talks, and workshops. Group discussions, mock interviews, and personality development. Aptitude training and quiz competitions. (b) Internship & Placement Club (Stimulus Internship & Placement Cell) Faculty Coordinator :- Prof H K Mahiyar , Er Vivek Tiwari Aim :To strengthen industry connect and facilitate career opportunities . PG Students ( 2nd Year STR/TRE/ENV) Activities: Internship database, industry liaison, pre-placement talks. For Example: Creating and maintaining database of internship opportunities. Coordinating with alumni, industries, and training partners. Organizing pre-placement talks and training sessions. Acting as a liaison between students and Training & Placement Cell. (c) BIS (Manak) Club SC-5389 Faculty Coordinator : Er Vivek Tiwari Aim: To inculcate the practice of standard-based engineering for quality and safety. Activities: Awareness drives on IS codes, workshops, case studies, competitions. Awareness drives on IS codes, standards, and practices in civil engineering. Workshops and case studies on application of BIS standards in design & construction. Technical competitions based on BIS manuals. 1.4 Sports Club (Dhyan Chand Club) Faculty Coordinator :- Prof D Dohare, Prof Krishna Pratap Verma, Prof Namrata Khede Activities: Indoor & outdoor sports, tournaments, fitness initiatives. Organizing indoor and outdoor sports events. Departmental tournaments and inter-college competitions. Fitness awareness and recreation activities. Coach & Captain - Final Year Vice Captain -Pre Final Year Student Junior Manager - PG 1st Year( STR/TRE/Env) & Student Team Manager -PG 2nd Year ( STR/TRE/Env) Team Selection Committee - Students Nominated from Each Year having experience of playing any outdoor sports. Aim: To promote teamwork, discipline, fitness, and sportsmanship spirit. Governance Structure Patron: Head of the Department, Civil Engineering & Applied Mechanics. Faculty Coordinators: One faculty advisor for each club, nominated by HoD. Student Leadership: Student Mentor Advisor, PG 1st Year( STR/TRE/Env) & PG 2nd Year ( STR/TRE/Env) 1. President : Final year student. 2. Vice-President : Pre-final year student. 3. Secretary : Pre-final year student. (Responsible for documentation & communication) 4. Joint Secretary : 2nd Year. (02 Students, Responsible for documentation & communication). 5. Budget Advisory Secretary :---- 6. Media Coordinator :---- 7. Executive Members : Students nominated from UG 1st Year, 2nd Year, 3rd Year, 4th year, PG 1st Year( STR/TRE/Env) & PG 2nd Year ( STR/TRE/Env). Activities Conducted by departmental Clubs: S.NO. Name of the Professional Societies/Bodies/Chapters/ Clubs Name of the Event National/ International level Date of Event CAY(JULY 2024 -JUNE 2025) 1 CLUB STIMULUS SEMINAR: TECH TALK - VI 12/03/25 2 CLUB STIMULUS Webinar: Tech Talk - V with Civil Engineers 09 February 2025 3 CLUB STIMULUS Ayaam 2025 29/02/2025 and 01/03/2025 4 BIS STANDARD CLUB TECHNICAL QUIZ NATIONAL 16/12/2024 5 BIS STANDARD CLUB FIELD VISIT NATIONAL 24/7/2024 6 BIS STANDARD CLUB AWARENESS CAMPAIGNS (CONSUMER RIGHTS AND SAFETY) NATIONAL 19/12/2024 CAYM1(JULY 2023 -JUNE 2024) 1 CLUB TRIVIM CIVIL TUSSLE 2024 2 CLUB STIMULUS Technocraft – 3D Visualization Workshop 10/02/2024 3 CLUB STIMULUS Ayaam 2024 21/02/2024 and 22/02/2024 4 BIS STANDARD CLUB STANDARD WRITING COMPETITION NATIONAL 28/12/2023 5 BIS STANDARD CLUB TECHNICAL QUIZ NATIONAL 7/3/2024 6 BIS STANDARD CLUB ROAD SAFETY MANAGEMENT SYSTEM NATIONAL 15/4/2024 CAYM2 (JULY 2022 -JUNE 2023) 1 CLUB TRIVIM TECHNIFICATION 2023 Feb 2023 CAYM3(JULY 2021 -JUNE 2022) 1 CLUB STIMULUS SAMANJASYA 24/10/2022 2 CLUB STIMULUS Tech Talk 3 (Webinar Session) 11/05/2022 EVENTS GALLARY AUTOCAD : No. of students Certified 350+ STAAD PRO : No. Of Certified Students 100+ REVIT : No. Of Certified Students 30+. TECHNOCRAFT ( workshops ): No. Of Certified Students 40 S AMANAJASYA SAMANJASYA-prelude delivered a rewarding experience for our participants through an interactive quiz exploring ""Environmental Hazards from Various Industries."" The subsequent segment featured a compelling session led by an expert in Environmental Engineering. SAMANJASYA seamlessly combined wit and intellectual stimulation, ensuring the event was truly valuable. It skillfully crafted an enjoyable yet informative experience, equipping the students with the practical knowledge needed to thrive in industrial settings. " CIVIL TUSSLE 2024 Ayaam 2025 TECH TALK VI Stimulus CEAMD hosted Tech Talk VI featuring IES Officer Vineet Hariyale, who shared insights on UPSC ESE preparation. Held at LT002 on 12th March 2025, the session guided civil engineering aspirants on strategy, motivation, and balancing studies, empowering them toward a successful and impactful career in engineering services.
